pattern: A mirror-symmetric tree reads the same flipped left-to-right — compare the left subtree against the right subtree as mirror images, outer child against outer child.
hints: Symmetry is about the two halves of the tree being mirror images of each other.
Compare the left and right subtrees as a pair, matching the left's left child with the right's right child.
Two subtrees mirror when their roots are equal and (left.left vs right.right) and (left.right vs right.left) both mirror.

edge_inputs.description: mirror shape but the inner values break symmetry
values mirror but the structure does not

stress.description: build a random left half and mirror it, then sometimes corrupt one value
stress.note: root always present; half the time symmetric, half the time broken
